text:
The Pirate Footballer Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 157 is a death row convict. His sentence was changed to a life sentence in an unconventional prison—Greed Island—together with a dozen other death row convicts. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 161 

Appearance

The Pirate Footballer wears the standard clothing for Razor's Pirates. He has a square-shaped head, a prominent chin, long and thin eyebrows, big ears, and a rectangular-shaped nose. 

Personality

The Pirate Footballer acts as if he's a simple non-player character (NPC) bot that simply follows the commands programmed into him but as it turns out he's really a human being serving a life sentence on Greed Island. 

Plot

Greed Island arc

His event during the "14 Devil challenge" is a soccer juggling contest in which one can attack the opponent with Nen while keeping the ball from touching the floor. Biscuit challenges him, but when the game begins, she deliberately loses. 

Later, when the group decides which sport to play, Hisoka says that he wants to soccer juggle since his Bungee Gum will simplify things. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 16, Chapter 160 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Like all the pirates working with Razor, he must be a decently capable Nen user, able to single-handedly defeat Greed Island players.

Manga Appearances

Competitions

Trivia

Anime and Manga Differences

In the 2011 anime adaptation, the Pirate footballer was juggling one of the balls with his head while holding the other under his armpit when Bisky stepped up to challenge him in soccer juggling. In the manga, he was juggling one of the balls with his feet while the other was balanced on his index finger.
Also in the 2011 anime adaptation, the character throws one of the soccer balls to Bisky towards the end of his explanation. In the manga, the ball was thrown before the explanation even began.
